title: ctenodon marginatus
parts: Ctenodon marginatus has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and stems, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of the Ctenodon marginatus plant contain flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are known to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The roots of the plant contain terpenoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory disorders. The stems of the plant contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which are used to treat digestive issues and skin problems.
side_effects: Ctenodon marginatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to stomach pain, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can cause liver and kidney damage, as well as interact with certain medications. Additionally, improper use of the plant can cause respiratory problems, including bronchitis and asthma-like symptoms in susceptible individuals.
